channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kurdish asylum seeker's 13-year detention struggle in Manus Island
item.description: Hatam Yekta, a 36-year-old Kurdish asylum seeker from Iran, has spent 13 years in offshore detention on Manus Island, his health now critically compromised. The article highlights his journey, which b...

item.title: Zimbabwe Government Supports Returnees with Reintegration Programs
item.description: The Zimbabwean government has announced comprehensive measures to assist returning citizens from South Africa, focusing on skill identification, job placement, and reintegration support. Minister Dani...

item.title: Alberta and Saskatchewan Begin Using Strychnine for Gopher Control After Delays
item.description: The use of strychnine for gopher control has officially started in Alberta and Saskatchewan after significant delays. Farmers can now use the controversial rodenticide to manage Richardson's ground sq...

item.title: Kalenjin and Kikuyu Elders to Hold Peace Talks Before 2027 Election
item.description: Rigathi Gachagua, leader of the Democracy for the Citizens Party (DCP), has called on Kikuyu and Kalenjin elders to hold urgent peace talks ahead of the 2027 general election. The meeting aims to prev...

item.title: Former U.S. Attorney General Todd Blanche's Controversial Statement on Epstein Survivors Draws Criticism from Legal Expert
item.description: During a Senate hearing, U.S. Attorney General nominee Todd Blanche faced scrutiny over his claim that he hadn't met with survivors of the Jeffrey Epstein child trafficking case because they have lawy...

item.title: Meghalaya Expands Pineapple Markets Through Retail Partnerships
item.description: The Meghalaya Pineapple Festival, organized by the Department of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare, aims to boost market access for pineapple growers through partnerships with retailers and digital mar...

item.title: Ghana’s New Green City Plan Faces Challenges Amid Accra’s Planning Issues
item.description: The article discusses Ghana's ambitious initiative to create a new green city as a response to devastating floods that have plagued Accra. President John Mahama's plan involves relocating government m...

item.title: Man Arrested Over Social Media Threat to Nigel Farage
item.description: A man in his 20s has been arrested in south London on suspicion of sending threatening communications to a Member of Parliament following a social media post alleging an intent to shoot Nigel Farage. ...

item.title: Trump's False Claims About CHIPS Act Spark Outrage Among Critics
item.description: President Donald Trump made unfounded allegations about the 2022 bipartisan CHIPS Act during a defense summit, claiming the legislation provided billions to companies that failed to utilize the funds ...

item.title: Senate President Threatens Legal Action Over Persistent Audio System Failures in National Assembly Chambers
item.description: Senate President Godswill Akpabio has threatened legal action against the contractor responsible for renovating the National Assembly’s chambers after repeated failures in the microphone system disrup...

item.title: Unisa data allowance suspension impacts thousands of students
item.description: The University of South Africa (Unisa) has faced criticism after suspending its monthly data allowance for distance learning students, leaving over 93,000 learners without essential internet access. S...

item.title: Cold weather drives up egg prices in Tanzania
item.description: Cold weather has significantly impacted egg production in Tanzania, leading to higher prices in key markets. Poultry farmers in Iringa Region report reduced laying rates due to the cold, creating a su...

item.title: Feral Cats Threaten Endemic Species in Mount Apo's Ecosystem
item.description: The article discusses the growing threat posed by stray and feral cats to the endemic wildlife of Mount Apo, a protected area. These non-native predators, introduced through historical maritime trade,...

item.title: Senate Orders NAFDAC and Ogun-Osun Basin to Reconcile Financial Accounts by July 25
item.description: The Senate Committee on Finance has directed the National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) and the Ogun-Osun River Basin Development Authority (OORBDA) to resolve financial...

item.title: Jillian Segal's Stance on Antisemitism Reporting and Israel's Role in Media Coverage
item.description: The article critiques Jillian Segal, Australia's antisemitism envoy, for her continued defense of Israel and questionable arguments during her appearance before the Royal Commission into Antisemitism ...

item.title: Argentina Overcomes Early Deficit to Beat England in World Cup Semi-Final
item.description: In a thrilling World Cup semi-final clash, Argentina staged a remarkable comeback against England to secure their place in the final. The match, held at a packed stadium, saw Argentina trail early in ...

item.title: Christopher Nolan's epic adaptation of Homer's Odyssey: a cinematic triumph
item.description: Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's 'The Odyssey' is a monumental achievement in cinema, blending ancient myth with modern spectacle. The film, starring Matt Damon as Odysseus, Anne Hathaway as ...

item.title: Kahama Market Fire Causes Significant Losses for Traders
item.description: A fierce morning fire on July 15, 2026, gutted 32 market stalls in Kahama's Dodoma Industrial Area, Shinyanga Region, causing heavy losses to 36 timber traders. The blaze, which started at 6 am, sprea...

item.title: Dogs intervene to protect women from alleged attacker at Kooyong Oval
item.description: A dramatic incident unfolded at Kooyong Oval in Victoria, where a pack of dogs reportedly intervened to prevent an alleged attacker from harming two women. Witnesses described the chaotic scene, with ...

item.title: Blue singer Lee Ryan convicted of racially aggravated assault on air steward
item.description: Lee Ryan, former member of the boyband Blue, is set to face sentencing for a racially aggravated assault on an air steward following the rejection of his appeal. The incident occurred during a flight ...
